name: Morphine
executiveSummary: Acute and chronic pain, moderate or severe. Symptomatic treatment of dyspnea. Dyspnea and cyanosis crises. Strong opioid.
indications:
## Main indications

Acute and chronic pain, moderate or severe. Symptomatic treatment of dyspnea. Dyspnea and cyanosis crises. Strong opioid.

> Adjust according to institutional protocol and medical prescription.

adulto.dose: 2–5 mg IV every 5–15 min PRN; PCA or infusion per prescription.

pediatrico.presentation: Ampoules: 10 mg/ml; Syrup (compounded): 0,1% (1 mg/ml), 0,3% (3 mg/ml), 1% (10 mg/ml) See formulation
pediatrico.infusionRate: Bolus over 5 min. 15 to 30 min with infusion pump.
pediatrico.dose: PO: Initial dose 0,1 mg/kg every 4 h; IV; SC; IM: < 50 kg: 0,05 mg/kg every 4 h; Continuous infusion: 0,05 mg/kg/hour; >50 kg: Initial dose PO: 10 mg every 4 h (no ceiling dose); IV: 5 mg every 4 h, Continuous infusion: 2 mg/hour. Increase dose by 50% each time until effective dose is reached.
pediatrico.notes: Association with an NSAID is recommended. Remember that neuropathic pain responds only partially to opioids (add adjuvants). Analgesic equivalence: 1 mg morphine IV = 2 mg morphine PO; 1 mg morphine IV = 10 µg fentanyl. See practical guide for analgosedation management and weaning in intermediate and moderate care units. For the 1% (10 mg/ml) presentation, the prescription must be endorsed on the reverse.

neonatal.dose: 0,01–0,05 mg/kg/dose according to NICU pain scale.

stability:
## General

- Use dilution within the shift.

## Pediatric guide

- Not specified in guide.
adverseEffects:
## Adverse effects

See opioid analgesics section.
